skill.name: use-skills
skill.description: Helps choose relevant installed skills for complex requests and briefly shows the working set before answering.
skill.summary: Coordinates installed skills into a focused working set for complex AI tasks.
skill.author: CyrusSE

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these are markdown inline-code markers and ordinary instruction words, not executable code. No shell execution, cryptographic implementation, system reconnaissance, network access, or data exfiltration was found. The remaining concern is low risk: the skill uses forceful workflow instructions that can change assistant behavior when deliberately invoked.

low_findings.title: Forceful Workflow Control Instructions
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to make the next response only a mode menu when invoked and to avoid file inspection or tool use before that choice. This is not malicious by itself, but it can interrupt normal task flow if installed or invoked accidentally.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit and behavior-changing, but it is scoped to intentional use of the skill. I found no evidence that it attempts to override system instructions, hide activity, or exfiltrate data.

content.user_title: Coordinate Skills for Complex AI Tasks
content.value_statement: Complex requests often need more than one installed skill, but choosing too many creates noise. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to select a small working set and explain it briefly.
actual_capabilities: Selects relevant installed skills for requests that span multiple domains.
Supports All related, Recommended, and Restricted selection modes.
Requires a mode choice when the user invokes the skill without one.
Defines a compact response block that lists mode, selected skills, and purpose.
Provides conflict handling rules for combining multiple skill instructions.
Includes quality checks for keeping skill use focused and nonduplicative.
limitations: It does not discover unavailable skills beyond the visible installed skill list.
It does not execute tools, read files, or inspect projects by itself.
It can add an extra mode-selection step before the main task starts.
Its value depends on the quality and relevance of installed skills.

use_cases.title: Plan Cross-Functional Work
use_cases.description: Choose planning, implementation, review, and documentation skills for a task that spans several work types.

use_cases.title: Control Skill Scope
use_cases.description: Use Restricted mode when a request should involve only the strongest matching skills.

use_cases.title: Improve Composite Outputs
use_cases.description: Combine writing, structure, testing, and review guidance into one coherent answer.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Selection
prompt_templates.scenario: Choose skills for a mixed task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Recommended mode to select skills for this request: plan the change, update the code, and include testing notes.

prompt_templates.title: Focused Selection
prompt_templates.scenario: Limit skill use to the strongest matches.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Restricted mode and only the strongest skills. Review this change and give the most important findings first.

prompt_templates.title: Broad Coverage
prompt_templates.scenario: Explore all meaningfully related support skills.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use All related mode for this request. Rewrite the README, improve structure, and note any product or developer experience gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Complex Delivery Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Coordinate multiple work streams in one answer.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Recommended mode. Turn this feature idea into an implementation plan with design, testing, documentation, and review considerations.

output_examples.input: Use Recommended mode to plan a feature and include testing notes.
output_examples.output: The assistant starts with a short mode block, lists the selected skills, and then gives one integrated implementation plan.

output_examples.input: Use Restricted mode to review this change.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects only the strongest review-related skills and presents findings before secondary context.

output_examples.input: Invoke the skill without naming a mode.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks the user to choose All related, Recommended, or Restricted before continuing.

best_practices: Name the desired mode when you already know the scope.
Use Recommended mode for most tasks that need balanced skill support.
Reserve All related mode for broad planning, restructuring, or exploratory work.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill when one domain skill is clearly enough.
Do not use All related mode for narrow bug fixes or simple answers.
Do not expect the skill to find skills that are not installed or visible.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps the assistant choose and combine installed skills for complex requests.

faq.question: Does it run commands or scripts?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed file contains instructions only and no executable script.

faq.question: Why are there three modes?
faq.answer: The modes let users choose broad, balanced, or restricted skill involvement.

faq.question: When should I use Recommended mode?
faq.answer: Use it when a request needs several skills but should stay focused.

faq.question: Can it inspect my installed skills?
faq.answer: It relies on visible or provided skill metadata. It does not discover hidden skills by itself.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found low workflow-control risk and no malicious behavior.
